steps.action: Azonto is a dance and music genre from Ghana with pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Azonto is a dance and music genre from Ghana with pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is connected to the traditional Ga dance Kpan
steps.text: It is connected to the traditional Ga dance Kpanlogo, associated with the coastal towns in the country such as Chorkor, James Town, Teshie, Nungua and Tema, in the Greater Accra Region.

steps.action: The Apaa dance, hence Azonto dance, involves a s
steps.text: The Apaa dance, hence Azonto dance, involves a set of hand movements that either mimic everyday activities especially ones concerning people's livelihoods, or moves that are meant to amuse an audience.

steps.action: For the basics of the dance, one foot is stiff a
steps.text: For the basics of the dance, one foot is stiff and planted, while the other foot is on its ball to allow the dancer to pivot and twist.

steps.action: The dancer's shoulders are slouched, and their h
steps.text: The dancer's shoulders are slouched, and their head is turned to one side with a "defiant and flirtatious" smirk on their face.

steps.action: The hands of the dancer point downwards and move
steps.text: The hands of the dancer point downwards and move in flowing, circular motions around themselves and the space in front of them, changing in levels from the head to the waist.

steps.action: It began with one- or two-step movements but has
steps.text: It began with one- or two-step movements but has been advanced to more complex and almost acrobatic movements Just like most African dances, Azonto involves knee bending and hip movements.

steps.action: The dance has effectively evolved from a few bas
steps.text: The dance has effectively evolved from a few basic moves to miming actions such as ironing of clothes, washing, driving, boxing, praying, swimming, and others.

steps.action: The azonto dance has since grown further to rela
steps.text: The azonto dance has since grown further to relay coded messages.

steps.action: The dance later got into the minds of most Ghana
steps.text: The dance later got into the minds of most Ghanaians.

steps.action: In 2013, most Ghanaian music videos were full of
steps.text: In 2013, most Ghanaian music videos were full of Azonto dance and later spread to most African countries and other parts of the world.

steps.action: Other Ghanians shared homemade Azonto dancing vi
steps.text: Other Ghanians shared homemade Azonto dancing videos and shared them to YouTube, prompting other users to comment on, praise, and critique their dancing.

steps.action: The success of "U Go Kill Me", increasing popula
steps.text: The success of "U Go Kill Me", increasing popularity of Azonto dancing, and the community created for Azonto dancing encouraged other artists to create their own music in the genre.

steps.action: Several accounts say Azonto was formerly known a
steps.text: Several accounts say Azonto was formerly known as 'Apaa' in these communities.

steps.action: Apaa was used to show the profession of an indiv
steps.text: Apaa was used to show the profession of an individual.

steps.action: Popular music researcher Jesse Weaver Shipley cl
steps.text: Popular music researcher Jesse Weaver Shipley claims that like hiplife, the popularity of Azonto is a direct result of its interactions in diaspora.

steps.action: Within a few months, "both received several hund
steps.text: Within a few months, "both received several hundred thousand hits" and were shared widely on Twitter and Facebook.
